Domanda: What is the fastest animal in the world?
Curiosità: It can exceed 320 km/h.

Domanda: Which animal kills the most humans every year?
Curiosità: Diseases transmitted by mosquitoes kill about 725,000 people a year.

Domanda: What is the only mammal capable of true flight?
Curiosità: Flying squirrels only glide, they don't truly fly.

Domanda: How many legs does an ant have?
Curiosità: As an insect, it has six legs.

Domanda: Which animal has the most powerful venom?
Curiosità: The venom of a single specimen can kill several humans.

Domanda: Which animal can regenerate lost limbs?
Curiosità: It can completely regenerate an arm and part of its body.

Domanda: Which land animal has the longest tongue relative to its body?
Curiosità: Its tongue can exceed the length of its own body.

Domanda: What can an eel do?
Opzione B: It can fly for short distances.
Curiosità: It can move on land, even for many kilometres.

Domanda: Where do sloths live?
Curiosità: Sloths live in the rainforests and tropical forests of Central and South America.

Domanda: What is the beaver's tail also called?
Curiosità: Named so because it resembles a rowing paddle.

Domanda: Who leads the elephant herd?
Opzione D: All elephants take turns leading the herd
Curiosità: The elephant herd is led by the matriarch, the oldest and most experienced female in the group.

Domanda: How long can the metamorphosis from caterpillar to butterfly last?
Curiosità: The metamorphosis can last up to eight years! As in the case of Eriogaster lanestris.

Domanda: Which statement is false?
Opzione A: Gorillas barely need to drink water
Opzione B: Gorillas feed almost exclusively on plants
Curiosità: Cultivated bananas are too sweet for their natural diet.

Domanda: Where are lion cubs born?
Opzione C: At the centre of the pride
Opzione D: In a hiding place, far from the pride
Curiosità: Only after about six weeks does the mother bring the cubs back to the pride.

Domanda: Which of these animals does not view humans as prey?
Curiosità: Even though they are at the top of the food chain, orcas do not consider humans a food source.

Domanda: How big are giant pandas at birth?
Opzione A: About the size of a cat
Opzione B: About the size of a dog
Opzione C: About the size of a hamster
Opzione D: About the size of a pig
Curiosità: At birth, panda cubs are tiny. They are barely the size of a hamster.

Domanda: Where do penguins live?
Opzione C: At both the South Pole and the North Pole
Curiosità: Penguins live exclusively in the Southern Hemisphere, mainly at the South Pole.

Domanda: What do hedgehogs NOT eat?
Curiosità: Hedgehogs eat insects, earthworms, eggs, and dead animal remains, but not grass.

Domanda: Why do snakes stick out their tongues?
Curiosità: They don't use their tongue for taste, but as a refined sensory organ to collect scent particles.

Domanda: What does a shark NOT eat?
Curiosità: Most sharks feed on all inhabitants of the sea.

Domanda: Among these animals, which is the largest?
Curiosità: The tiger is the largest living big cat, with males that can reach 300 kg.

Domanda: Which colour is particularly difficult to see in the ocean depths?
Curiosità: Only a few deep-sea animals can see red.

Domanda: What colour is a polar bear's skin?
Curiosità: The skin is black to absorb solar heat; the fur is transparent.

Domanda: Which animal can sleep for up to 3 years?
Curiosità: Some species of snails can do so to survive drought.

Domanda: Which of these animals cannot jump?
Curiosità: Due to its bone structure and weight, the elephant is the only mammal that cannot jump.

Domanda: Which bird lays the largest egg relative to its own body?
Curiosità: The egg can weigh up to 25% of the female's body weight.

Domanda: What is a group of lions called?
Curiosità: From the English word 'Pride'.

Domanda: Which animal has fingerprints similar to human ones?
Curiosità: They are so similar that they can confuse forensic investigators.

Domanda: Which animal has the most powerful bite?
Curiosità: With its force, nearly five times greater than that of the great white shark.

Domanda: What is the loudest animal in the world (can reach 188 decibels)?
Curiosità: Its song can be heard by other whales up to 800 km away.

Domanda: Which country did the kiwi fruit originally come from?
Curiosità: Originally called 'Chinese gooseberry'.

Domanda: What is the only food that never expires?
Curiosità: Found still edible in Egyptian tombs.

Domanda: Which spice is the most expensive in the world by weight?
Curiosità: It takes about 150,000 crocus flowers to produce 1 kg.

Domanda: Which type of pasta means 'little worms'?
Curiosità: The name comes from their thin shape.

Domanda: Which fruit has its seeds on the outside?
Curiosità: The strawberry is the only 'fruit' with its seeds on the skin.

Domanda: In which country was Hawaiian pizza (with pineapple) invented?
Curiosità: Created in Ontario in 1962 by a Greek restaurateur.

Domanda: Which vitamin is produced by the body with sun exposure?
Curiosità: It is essential for calcium absorption.

Domanda: What is the most stolen item in supermarkets?
Curiosità: Stolen for resale on black markets or restaurants due to its high value per kg.

Domanda: What is the most consumed food in the world?
Curiosità: It is the staple food for more than half of the world's population.

Domanda: Which food never sinks in water because it contains 25% air?
Curiosità: Thanks to the air between its cells, apples float perfectly.

Domanda: Which vegetable has the highest water content (96%)?
Curiosità: Eating a cucumber is almost the equivalent of drinking a glass of water.

Domanda: Which fizzy drink originally contained cocaine?
Curiosità: It was completely removed from the recipe only in 1903.

Domanda: What is the hottest chilli pepper in the world?
Curiosità: It exceeds 2 million Scoville units.

Domanda: What is the most expensive cheese in the world?
Curiosità: Made from donkey milk.

Domanda: What is the origin of the famous 'Fortune Cookie'?
Curiosità: They were invented in California at the beginning of the 1900s; in China they are almost unknown.

Domanda: Which country consumes the most pizza per capita in the world?
Curiosità: In relation to population, Norwegians consume more pizza than any other nation.

Domanda: What is the best-selling and most loved ice cream flavour globally?
Curiosità: Vanilla is consistently in first place for its versatility in pairings.

Domanda: Which of these pasta shapes does not exist?
Curiosità: Pipe and Zitoni are short shapes, Trenette is a long shape.

Domanda: Which of these foods has the most calories per 100g?
Curiosità: Per 100g, coconut is by far the most caloric, with about 365 kcal.

Domanda: Which country produces the most wine in the world?
Curiosità: Italy is usually in first place for global wine production.

Domanda: Many believe it was born abroad, but which is a 100% Italian dish?
Curiosità: Often mistaken for American, it was born in Rome in 1908 at the restaurant of Alfredo di Lelio.

Domanda: Among the MasterChef Italia judges, who holds the greatest number of Michelin stars?
Curiosità: Bruno Barbieri, after Enrico Bartolini, is the second Italian with the most stars.

Domanda: Which country eats the most pizza per capita?
Curiosità: Norwegians consume extremely high quantities.

Domanda: What is the best-selling fast food in the world?
Curiosità: Billions sold every year.

Domanda: In which city was 'Cotoletta alla Milanese' invented?
Curiosità: Documents from 1148 already mention the 'lombolum cum panitio' in Milan.

Domanda: What makes the famous 'Black Spaghetti' black?
Curiosità: The cuttlefish ink sac is burst directly into the sauce during cooking.

Domanda: Which region claims the origin of 'Nduja?
Curiosità: It is a spicy spreadable salami typical of Spilinga.

Domanda: Which of these is NOT an ingredient in the original Tiramisù?
Curiosità: The original Treviso recipe includes eggs, sugar, mascarpone, coffee and savoiardi (no cream!).

Domanda: Which Emilian condiment must age at least 12 years to be 'Traditional'?
Curiosità: Traditional Balsamic Vinegar of Modena DOP requires a minimum ageing of 12 or 25 years.

Domanda: What is 'Finocchiona'?
Curiosità: It is a typical Tuscan cured meat containing fennel seeds in the mixture.

Domanda: What is the best-selling manga of all time?
Curiosità: One Piece by Eiichiro Oda is the best-selling manga of all time, with over 500 million copies.

Domanda: What is the highest-rated TV series according to IMDb?
Curiosità: Breaking Bad has a rating of 9.5/10 on IMDb.

Domanda: What is the highest-rated film according to IMDb?
Curiosità: The Shawshank Redemption (1994) holds first place with 9.3/10.

Domanda: Which actor holds the record for the most Oscars won as Best Actor?
Curiosità: Daniel Day-Lewis is the only man to have won three.

Domanda: What is the longest-running anime in history, with over 7,000 episodes?
Curiosità: On air since 1969, Sazae-san is in the Guinness World Records.

Domanda: How many minutes does the film 'Titanic' (1997) last?
Curiosità: The 1912 scenes last exactly 2 hours and 40 minutes, the real time of the sinking.

Domanda: Which horror film holds the record for the most audience members fainting in cinemas?
Curiosità: In 1973, The Exorcist caused such strong reactions that many cinemas provided vomit bags.

Domanda: What is the title of the Studio Ghibli film that won the Oscar in 2003?
Curiosità: It was the first non-English language film to win the Oscar for Best Animated Feature.

Domanda: Which iconic movie villain appears on screen for only 16 minutes?
Curiosità: In 'The Silence of the Lambs', Anthony Hopkins won the Oscar for Best Actor.

Domanda: In the film 'Interstellar', what does each sound tick on Miller's planet correspond to?
Curiosità: Hans Zimmer inserted a tick every 1.25 seconds, representing one week on Earth.

Domanda: For 'The Lord of the Rings', how many pairs of Hobbit feet were made?
Curiosità: Each pair was 'single-use' and had to be glued on every morning.

Domanda: In which Disney film is the phrase 'People always do crazy things when they're in love' said?
Curiosità: Megara says this line while talking to Hades.

Domanda: In the film 'The Lion King', the lions' roar is from which animal?
Curiosità: The lion's roar wasn't 'powerful' enough, so they used tiger roars.

Domanda: Which of the following lines is NOT said in Shrek?
Opzione B: I don't have a problem with the world
Opzione D: You don't meet a donkey riding a horse every day
Curiosità: The last line is said by Aladdin.

Domanda: Which of these stars never appeared in the series Friends?
Curiosità: Jim Carrey never made a cameo in Friends.

Domanda: What is the highest-grossing film in history?
Curiosità: It holds the record with over 2.9 billion dollars.

Domanda: How many Oscars did 'The Return of the King' win?
Curiosità: It won in every category in which it was nominated.

Domanda: Who plays Jack Sparrow?
Curiosità: The role earned him an Oscar nomination.

Domanda: What is the name of the Simpsons' city?
Curiosità: Chosen because it is one of the most common city names in the USA.

Domanda: In The Matrix, what colour is the truth pill?
Curiosità: The blue one keeps you in the illusion.

Domanda: Which actor played James Bond in 7 official films?
Curiosità: Connery did 6 official ones and one unofficial one.

Domanda: What was the first Disney animated feature film?
Curiosità: Released in 1937.

Domanda: Who directed 'Inception'?
Curiosità: Known for his complex and non-linear plots.

Domanda: Who was named 'Most Handsome Man in the World' in 2016?
Curiosità: In 2016 the magazine People awarded him as Sexiest Man Alive.

Domanda: What is the most-watched Netflix series of all time?
Curiosità: Stranger Things has over 1.2 billion total views.

Domanda: What is the most-watched YouTube video of all time?
Curiosità: It has over 10 billion views.

Domanda: Which Disney film was the first to be nominated for the Oscar for Best Picture?
Curiosità: In 1991 it was a historic milestone for animation.

Domanda: Which of these actors won a posthumous Oscar?
Curiosità: He won Best Supporting Actor for 'The Dark Knight'.

Domanda: What is the film that generated the most profit relative to its budget?
Curiosità: Made for $15,000, it grossed 193 million. It is considered the most profitable film in history.

Domanda: Which film holds the record for the most profanities (over 500 F-words)?
Curiosità: The word is spoken on average 3 times per minute.

Domanda: What is the most consumed drink in the world after water?
Curiosità: Tea beats coffee in global consumption volume.

Domanda: What colour is an aircraft's 'Black Box'?
Curiosità: It is fluorescent orange to be easily traceable.

Domanda: How many keys does a standard piano have?
Curiosità: 52 white keys and 36 black keys.

Domanda: In which language was 'The Little Prince' written?
Curiosità: Written by Antoine de Saint-Exupéry in 1943.

Domanda: What is the most abundant chemical element in the universe?
Curiosità: It makes up about 75% of the mass of ordinary matter.

Domanda: What does the acronym 'LEGO' mean?
Curiosità: From the Danish 'Leg Godt'.

Domanda: What is the social network with the most users in the world?
Curiosità: Facebook still holds the top spot with about 3 billion users.

Domanda: How many hearts does an octopus have?
Curiosità: Two hearts pump blood to the gills, one to the rest of the body.

Domanda: In which city is the busiest airport in the world located?
Curiosità: Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta Airport almost always holds the top spot.

Domanda: How many times a day does an average adult laugh?
Curiosità: Children laugh over 300 times.

Domanda: How much skin do we shed on average every year?
Curiosità: About 4 kg of dead skin every year.

Domanda: How many people have lived on Earth in total throughout history?
Curiosità: About 7% of all humans ever born are alive today.

Domanda: How many bones does an adult have?
Curiosità: More than half of the bones are in the hands and feet.

Domanda: How much does an average cloud weigh?
Curiosità: An average-sized cloud (cumulus) weighs about 500 tonnes.

Domanda: What is the best-selling video game in history?
Curiosità: Minecraft is the best-selling video game of all time, with over 300 million copies.

Domanda: What is the best-selling object in the world?
Curiosità: The BIC Cristal ballpoint pen has sold over 100 billion units.

Domanda: What is the most popular colour in the world?
Curiosità: Blue is the favourite colour in numerous international surveys.

Domanda: What is the most used password in the world?
Curiosità: '123456' can be cracked in less than a second.

Domanda: What is the most common fear?
Curiosità: Glossophobia affects about 75% of the population.

Domanda: How many languages exist in the world?
Curiosità: About 7,000 languages are spoken in the world.

Domanda: What is the most widespread name in the world?
Curiosità: The most widespread name is Muhammad, due to the very wide spread of Islam.

Domanda: What is the most lasting memory in the human brain?
Curiosità: The sense of smell is directly connected to the emotional areas of the brain.

Domanda: What is the most widespread profession in the world?
Curiosità: Billions of people work in agriculture.

Domanda: What was the original colour of carrots?
Curiosità: The orange colour was created by the Dutch in the 17th century to honour the House of Orange.

Domanda: What is the most lost item by passengers on the London Underground?
Curiosità: Every year, hundreds of dentures are found in the lost property offices!

Domanda: What is the most used word in the world after 'Hello'?
Curiosità: 'OK' is universally recognised in almost every language on the planet.

Domanda: What is the happiest country in the world according to the World Happiness Report?
Curiosità: Finland consistently wins first place for several years.

Domanda: Which Italian region has the greatest number of UNESCO World Heritage Sites?
Curiosità: Lombardy holds the record with 10 recognised sites.

Domanda: Which Italian university is the oldest in the Western world?
Curiosità: The University of Bologna (Alma Mater Studiorum) was founded in 1088.

Domanda: How many regions does Italy have?
Curiosità: Of these, 5 have special autonomous status.

Domanda: What is the best-selling technological product of all time?
Curiosità: It surpassed 155 million units.

Domanda: What is the best-selling car in history?
Curiosità: Over 50 million sold.

Domanda: What is the most stolen painting in history?
Curiosità: It has been stolen multiple times.

Domanda: What is the best-selling toy of all time?
Curiosità: LEGO bricks have been produced since 1949.

Domanda: Which Italian region has the most castles?
Curiosità: Over 700 castles recorded.

Domanda: Which Italian region has the most municipalities?
Curiosità: It has over 1,500 municipalities.

Domanda: Which Italian city has the most bridges?
Curiosità: Over 400 bridges.

Domanda: Which region produces the most wine in Italy?
Curiosità: Veneto is first in terms of quantity.

Domanda: What is the most visited city in Italy?
Curiosità: It welcomes millions of tourists every year.

Domanda: Which European country drinks the most alcohol per capita?
Curiosità: Beer is part of the local culture.

Domanda: Which multinational is the world's largest toy distributor?
Curiosità: With Happy Meals, McDonald's distributes over 1.5 billion toys a year.

Domanda: How many elements are currently in the periodic table?
Curiosità: The heaviest element is Oganesson (atomic number 118).

Domanda: What is the largest organ in the human body?
Curiosità: The skin weighs on average between 3.5 and 5 kg and covers about 2 m².

Domanda: Which tennis player has won the most Slams in history?
Curiosità: Novak Djokovic leads with 24 Slams.

Domanda: What is the tallest building in the world (828 metres)?
Curiosità: It is located in Dubai and has over 160 floors.

Domanda: What do male penguins do to propose to a female?
Curiosità: They look for the smoothest and most beautiful pebble on the beach to give to their chosen one.

Domanda: Who is the author of the painting 'Guernica'?
Curiosità: Pablo Picasso created the monumental work in 1937.

Domanda: What is the most stolen car colour in the world?
Curiosità: White cars are easier to resell or disguise.

Domanda: Which Italian province has the highest quality of life?
Curiosità: Bologna often wins the Il Sole 24 Ore ranking.

Domanda: What is the most visited website in the world (excluding Google)?
Curiosità: YouTube is the second most visited site and the second search engine.

Domanda: What is the most expensive substance in the world per gram?
Curiosità: Producing a single gram of antimatter would cost about 62 trillion dollars.

Domanda: What is the most widespread surname on planet Earth?
Curiosità: Over 100 million people in the world are called Wang.

Domanda: Which country has the most official languages?
Curiosità: It has 11 official languages.

Domanda: Which country has the most islands?
Curiosità: Over 260,000 islands.

Domanda: Which is the youngest country in the world?
Curiosità: Independent since 2011.

Domanda: Which continent has the most deserts?
Curiosità: It is technically a cold desert.

Domanda: Which is the largest country in the world by surface area?
Curiosità: It spans 11 different time zones.

Domanda: In which city is the 'City of the Dead' located?
Curiosità: It is an area of Cairo where thousands of people live among the tombs.

Domanda: Which is the largest island in the world?
Curiosità: Despite its immense size, it has only 56,000 inhabitants.

Domanda: Which city is divided between two continents, Europe and Asia?
Curiosità: Divided by the Bosphorus Strait, it is the only metropolis on two continents.

Domanda: Which is the most populous country in the world?
Curiosità: India has surpassed the threshold of 1.4 billion people.

Domanda: 'Point Nemo' in the Pacific. What is its peculiarity?
Opzione D: A submerged pyramid is found there
Curiosità: It is the point furthest from any land (2,688 km).

Domanda: What is the only continent without deserts?
Curiosità: Europe is the only one without classic sandy deserts.

Domanda: In which sea is the island of Crete located?
Curiosità: It is the largest island in Greece and closes the Aegean Sea basin to the south.

Domanda: Which of the following cities is NOT in Emilia-Romagna?
Curiosità: Asola is a municipality in the province of Mantua in Lombardy.

Domanda: Which is the largest US state by surface area?
Curiosità: Alaska covers over 20% of the entire area of the United States.

Domanda: Which mountain range separates Europe from Asia?
Curiosità: The Ural Mountains are traditionally considered the geographical boundary.

Domanda: What is the capital of Australia?
Curiosità: Many confuse it with Sydney, which is actually the largest city.

Domanda: What is the longest river in the world?
Curiosità: The Nile measures about 6,650 km, although some recent research suggests the Amazon may be longer.

Domanda: In which country is Machu Picchu located?
Curiosità: Built by the Incas in the 15th century in the Andes.

Domanda: What is the highest mountain in the world?
Curiosità: Everest reaches 8,849 metres above sea level.

Domanda: How many stars does the US flag have?
Curiosità: One star per state. The last added was Hawaii in 1960.

Domanda: Which is the smallest country in the world?
Curiosità: It measures only 0.44 km² and is the smallest state in the world.

Domanda: What is the largest desert in the world?
Curiosità: Antarctica is technically a cold desert of 14 million km².

Domanda: On which continent is Kilimanjaro located?
Curiosità: Kilimanjaro is located in Tanzania and is the highest mountain in Africa (5,895 m).

Domanda: Which language is the most spoken in the world as a mother tongue?
Curiosità: Mandarin is spoken by over 900 million people as a mother tongue.

Domanda: In which country is the Parthenon located?
Curiosità: The Parthenon is in Athens on the Acropolis, built in the 5th century BC.

Domanda: What is the largest ocean in the world?
Curiosità: The Pacific covers about 46% of the total ocean surface.

Domanda: In which country are the Pyramids of Giza located?
Curiosità: The Pyramids of Giza are built on the west bank of the Nile, dating back to 2500 BC.

Domanda: How many countries share a border with Russia?
Curiosità: Russia shares borders with 14 countries, the highest number in the world.

Domanda: In which city is the Sagrada Família located?
Curiosità: The Sagrada Família is Antoni Gaudí's cathedral in Barcelona, still under construction.

Domanda: Which country has won the most Nobel Prizes per capita?
Curiosità: The USA leads the absolute ranking with over 400 Nobel Prizes.

Domanda: How long did the 'Hundred Years' War' last?
Curiosità: Fought between France and England from 1337 to 1453.

Domanda: Who was the first US president to live in the White House?
Curiosità: Washington oversaw the construction but never lived there.

Domanda: Which Roman emperor appointed his horse as a senator?
Curiosità: The horse was named Incitatus.

Domanda: In what year did the Titanic sink?
Curiosità: In the night between 14 and 15 April 1912.

Domanda: Which famous Egyptian queen was actually of Greek origin?
Curiosità: She descended from the Ptolemaic dynasty, of Macedonian origin.

Domanda: Which European country remained neutral during both World Wars?
Curiosità: Switzerland has maintained neutrality since 1815.

Domanda: Who invented the movable type printing press?
Curiosità: Gutenberg invented the printing press around 1440, revolutionising the spread of knowledge.

Domanda: In which year did the Western Roman Empire fall?
Curiosità: 476 AD is the conventional date of the fall, with the deposition of Romulus Augustulus.

Domanda: What was the name of the first artificial satellite launched into space?
Curiosità: Sputnik 1 was launched by the USSR on 4 October 1957.

Domanda: Who was the first man to walk on the Moon?
Curiosità: Neil Armstrong set foot on the Moon on 20 July 1969 during the Apollo 11 mission.

Domanda: In which year was the Magna Carta signed?
Curiosità: The Magna Carta was signed by King John of England in 1215, limiting royal power.

Domanda: Who was the leader of the Soviet Union during World War II?
Curiosità: Stalin led the USSR from 1924 to 1953.

Domanda: In which year did Columbus reach the Americas?
Curiosità: 12 October 1492 is the official date of the discovery of the Americas.

Domanda: Which revolution took place in France in 1789?
Curiosità: The storming of the Bastille on 14 July 1789 is the symbol of the French Revolution.

Domanda: Who painted the Mona Lisa?
Curiosità: Leonardo da Vinci painted the Mona Lisa between 1503 and 1519.

Domanda: In which year was Rome founded?
Curiosità: According to tradition, Rome was founded by Romulus on 21 April 753 BC.

Domanda: The American Civil War (1861–1865) ended with the defeat of which side?
Curiosità: The South (Confederates) surrendered on 9 April 1865 with the surrender of General Lee.

Domanda: Who was the first President of the Italian Republic?
Curiosità: Enrico De Nicola was the first President of the Italian Republic from 1948.

Domanda: In which year did Italy officially become a Republic?
Curiosità: On 2 June 1946 the institutional referendum was held.

Domanda: Which ancient city was destroyed by the eruption of Vesuvius in 79 AD?
Curiosità: Pompeii was buried under 4–6 metres of volcanic ash on 24 August 79 AD.

Domanda: Who was the first emperor of Rome?
Curiosità: Octavian Augustus became the first Roman emperor in 27 BC.

Domanda: Which instrument was invented by Antonio Meucci?
Curiosità: Antonio Meucci invented the telephone, but it was Alexander Graham Bell who patented it in 1876.

Domanda: How many years did the Trojan War last according to Greek mythology?
Curiosità: According to the Iliad, the Trojan War lasted 10 years.

Domanda: Who led the Russian Revolution of 1917?
Curiosità: Lenin led the Bolsheviks to the seizure of power in 1917.

Domanda: In which year did World War II begin?
Curiosità: On 1 September 1939, Germany invaded Poland.

Domanda: Which ancient people built the Pyramids of Giza?
Curiosità: The Pyramids were built by the Egyptians about 4,500 years ago as tombs for the pharaohs.

Domanda: Who was the dictator of Nazi Germany?
Curiosità: Adolf Hitler led Germany as Führer from 1933 to 1945.

Domanda: In which year did the Normandy landings take place?
Curiosità: D-Day, 6 June 1944, was the largest amphibious operation in history.

Domanda: Which great ancient library was burned in the 1st century BC?
Curiosità: The Library of Alexandria was the largest in the ancient world, destroyed between the 1st century BC and the 7th century AD.

Domanda: Who was the Pharaoh during the Exodus of the Jews from Egypt (according to the Bible)?
Curiosità: The majority of scholars identify Ramesses II as the Pharaoh of the Exodus.
